name: libsub-exporter-progressive-perl
title: libsub-exporter-progressive-perl
summary: module for using Sub::Exporter only if needed
description:
module for using Sub::Exporter only if needed
Sub::Exporter is an incredibly powerful module, but with that power comes
great responsibility, as well as some runtime penalties.
Sub::Exporter::Progressive is a Sub::Exporter wrapper that will let your
users just use Exporter if all they are doing is picking exports, but use
Sub::Exporter if your users try to use Sub::Exporter's more advanced
features, like renaming exports.
.
Note that this module will export @EXPORT and @EXPORT_OK package variables
for Exporter to work. Additionally, if your package uses advanced
Sub::Exporter features like currying, this module will only use
Sub::Exporter, so you might as well use it directly.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libsub-exporter-progressive-perl because module for using Sub::Exporter only if needed.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libsub-exporter-progressive-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libsub-exporter-progressive-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libsub-exporter-progressive-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
